tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read file contents. Returns full text with line numbers.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write content to file. Overwrites existing file. Only allowed in sandbox paths.

tools.name: list_files
tools.description: List files and directories in a path.

tools.name: search_in_files
tools.description: Search for text across files using grep/ripgrep.

tools.name: bash_exec
tools.description: Execute bash command. Only safe commands in whitelist allowed.

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Search user's memory (conversations, documents, knowledge base).

tools.name: cubesandbox_create
tools.description: Create a new KVM-isolated CubeSandbox microVM and register it under ``name``. Returns the VM's OpenCode HTTP URL for AI-native code edits. The VM has no access to host secrets (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.memory are physically unreachable). Optional: template_id, timeout, upload_dir (host directory seeded into the VM at create time).

name.description: Unique identifier for the VM (use this in subsequent cubesandbox_exec / cubesandbox_destroy calls)

template_id.description: CubeSandbox template id (default: CUBE_TEMPLATE_ID env var)

upload_dir.description: Host directory to seed into the VM (optional)

tools.name: cubesandbox_exec
tools.description: Execute a shell command inside the CubeSandbox microVM registered as ``name``. The command runs as the VM's default user with no host-filesystem access. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit_code. Call cubesandbox_create first.

name.description: VM identifier (must match a prior cubesandbox_create call)

command.description: Shell command to run inside the VM

tools.name: cubesandbox_list
tools.description: List the CubeSandbox microVMs the agentic process has live handles for. Returns name, sandbox_id, is_running, last_used_ts for each VM created via cubesandbox_create. This is the in-process view, not a CubeMaster-wide listing.

tools.name: cubesandbox_destroy
tools.description: Tear down the CubeSandbox microVM registered as ``name`` and release its resources. Idempotent: destroying a name that doesn't exist returns success=true. The VM's host secrets are never accessible, so destroy is safe to call at any point.

tools.name: cubesandbox_upload
tools.description: Upload a host directory into the CubeSandbox microVM registered as ``name``. Use this to push a cloned repo or build artifacts into the VM after cubesandbox_create.
